extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked solo with 50km remaining on gravel sectors, building 90+ seconds while ALAPHILIPPE cracked despite chasing from earlier crash
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR attacked at 50km on gravel, not on final climbs—positioning before finale mattered more than peak power.
ALAPHILIPPE's crash recovery effort at 100km mark destroyed his ability to respond 30km later despite reaching peloton.
ASGRIN dragged VALVERDE/SIMMONS on penultimate gravel sector but cooperation still lost 50+ seconds—chasing groups failed coordination.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: absorbed early crash yet soloed 50km including gravel sectors, suggesting superior durability/recovery in chaotic conditions
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: returned from 100km crash but cracked when ASGRIN accelerated on sector 3, showing energy depletion timeline matters
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: age 41, best-ever finish (2nd) working with ASGRIN late, proving diesel engine still functional in long attritional races
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Carlos: negative: bridged to POGAČAR initially but caught 25km later, young climbers lack staying power on extended gravel efforts
rider_signals.ASGRIN Jakob: positive: only rider forcing late chase on gravel sectors, showing Deceuninck tactical desperation creates aggressive domestiques
